response.justification: While Mohammed is a highly capable software engineer with 17+ years of experience and demonstrated performance optimization skills, the role requires specialized expertise in SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture analysis, and deep-learning workload optimization that is absent from his resume. His career has focused on web development, backend services, and DevOps infrastructure rather than the hardware-level design and accelerator optimization this Principal Performance Architect position demands.
strengths: 17+ years of professional software engineering with proven ability to optimize for performance and low-latency systems (Dunnhumby experience with async I/O, caching, fibers, and high-performance databases)
Technical depth across multiple languages and domains (web, mobile, embedded, DevOps) with principled engineering practices like TDD and code quality emphasis
Current exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and developer tooling at re:cinq, indicating awareness of AI systems and modern engineering patterns
concerns: No SoC or IP modeling experience—a critical required skill for this hardware-focused role, entirely absent from resume
No demonstrated expertise in deep learning workload optimization, neural network inference, or GPU/accelerator architecture analysis
Career trajectory entirely in software/web development and DevOps; lacks hardware-software co-design experience and systems-level hardware optimization background

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware design experience
Absence of deep learning and GPU/accelerator expertise
Strong performance optimization and low-latency systems experience
